pairs.subject: Mandatory Training Session
pairs.desc:
Dear Team,

We are implementing a new training session that is mandatory for all employees. This training will cover essential compliance topics and is scheduled for next Thursday at 10 AM in the main conference room. Please find the training materials attached.

Make sure to review the document before the session. Your participation is crucial to ensure we are all aligned with company policies.

Best regards,

HR Team
pairs.explain: This email comes from the HR department and contains a legitimate training attachment.

pairs.subject: Urgent: Update Your Training Materials
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

We have updated our training materials and you are required to download the new version immediately. Please find the updated training document attached. Failure to comply may result in disciplinary action.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

HR Department
pairs.explain: This email uses urgency and a suspicious attachment to trick the recipient into downloading malware.

pairs.subject: Quarterly Financial Report
pairs.desc:
Hello Team,

Attached is the quarterly financial report for your review. Please take a moment to go through it and let me know if you have any questions. We will discuss the findings in our upcoming meeting.

Thank you for your attention.

Best,

Finance Team
pairs.explain: This email is from the Finance department and contains a legitimate report attachment.

pairs.subject: Action Required: Review Your Invoice
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

Please find your invoice attached. It is imperative that you review and confirm the details as soon as possible. Failure to do so may result in service interruptions.

Best regards,

Finance Department
pairs.explain: This email contains a malicious attachment disguised as an invoice, leveraging urgency to prompt action.

pairs.subject: IT System Maintenance Notification
pairs.desc:
Dear All,

We would like to inform you that there will be scheduled maintenance on our IT systems this weekend. The systems will be down from Saturday at 10 PM until Sunday at 6 AM. Please save your work accordingly.

Thank you for your understanding.

Best,

IT Support
pairs.explain: This email is a standard notification from the IT department with no suspicious content.

pairs.subject: Immediate Action Required: Update Your Credentials
pairs.desc:
Dear User,

We have detected unusual activity on your account. Please download the attached file to update your credentials immediately. Failure to do so will result in account suspension.

Regards,

IT Department
pairs.explain: This email attempts to create fear and urgency while including a malicious attachment.

pairs.subject: Travel Policy Update
pairs.desc:
Dear Team,

We have updated our travel policy to better accommodate employee needs. Please find the new policy attached for your review. If you have any questions or concerns, feel free to reach out.

Best,

Travel Department
pairs.explain: This email is a legitimate communication from the Travel department with a valid attachment.

pairs.subject: Important: Confirm Your Travel Details
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

Your travel details have been updated. Please confirm your itinerary by downloading the attached document. It is crucial to complete this step to avoid any issues with your upcoming trip.

Thank you,

Travel Team
pairs.explain: This email uses urgency and a malicious attachment to deceive the recipient.

pairs.subject: Legal Document Review
pairs.desc:
Hello,

Attached you will find the legal documents that require your review. Please read through them and provide your feedback by the end of the week. Your input is valuable to ensure compliance.

Best,

Legal Team
pairs.explain: This email is from the Legal department with a legitimate document attached.

pairs.subject: Urgent: Review Your Legal Obligations
pairs.desc:
Dear Employee,

It is critical that you review your legal obligations immediately. Download the attached file and follow the instructions. Ignoring this could lead to serious consequences.

Sincerely,

Legal Department
pairs.explain: This email creates a sense of urgency and contains a malicious attachment to trick the recipient.
